title: Zentrade vs TradeZella: Which Is the Best Trading Journal for Prop Firms?

content.text: Choosing the right trading journal is one of the most impactful decisions a futures trader can make when going through a prop firm evaluation. The wrong tool means flying blind on your consistency metrics, missing patterns of self-sabotage, and repeating the same costly mistakes across evaluations. The two most-searched options in 2025 are Zentrade and TradeZella. This comparison covers every dimension that matters for funded traders: price, AI features, prop firm-specific tools, ease of use, and language support.

The short answer: for futures traders targeting funded accounts through FTMO, Apex Trader Funding, TopStep, Tradoverse, or Uprofit, Zentrade is the stronger choice. It was built specifically for this use case, costs less at every tier, and includes AI-powered revenge trading detection that TradeZella simply does not offer. TradeZella is a solid product — but it was designed for a broader audience of stock and options traders in English-speaking markets.
Zentrade vs TradeZella: Feature Comparison at a Glance

rows: Yes — NQ, ES, MES, MNQ, CL, GC preconfigured

content.text: This comparison is based on features available as of March 2026. TradeZella pricing and features may change — always verify at their official site before subscribing.
Pricing Comparison: Zentrade vs TradeZella

Price is the first filter for most traders in the evaluation phase. You're already paying $100–$600 for the evaluation itself, depending on account size. Adding an expensive journal tool before you're even funded creates unnecessary financial pressure.
Zentrade Pricing Breakdown

Zentrade has four tiers designed to scale with a trader's journey. The Free plan gives you 1 account with manual trade entry and a basic dashboard — no credit card required. The Starter plan at $9/month ($84/year) covers 2 accounts, CSV export, and the trade calendar. Professional at $29/month ($249/year) unlocks auto CSV import, equity curve, profit factor analytics, consistency tracking, and advanced filters. ZenMode at $59/month ($499/year) adds the full AI suite: revenge trading detection, weekly emotional performance reports, risk rule alerts, and prop firm benchmarking.
TradeZella Pricing Breakdown

TradeZella does not offer a meaningful free plan. Access to any real analytics requires a paid subscription starting at $29/month. This means that for the equivalent of Zentrade's $9 Starter plan — which gives you basic analytics and multi-account support — you would be paying over three times the price with TradeZella. Their AI-tier pricing is comparable to ZenMode at the top end, but without the revenge trading detection and emotional analysis features.

If you're mid-evaluation and just need the key metrics — consistency percentage, equity curve, and profit factor — Zentrade Professional at $29/month covers everything you need without paying for features you won't use during the evaluation phase.
Prop Firm-Specific Features: Where Zentrade Pulls Ahead

More than 85% of traders who attempt a prop firm evaluation fail. The most common reasons are not strategic — they're behavioral. Revenge trading after a losing streak, violating the consistency rule on a big winning day, or trading during historically bad hours. A trading journal built for prop firms should make these patterns impossible to miss.
Consistency Rule Tracking

The consistency rule is misunderstood by most traders going through their first evaluation. In simple terms: no single trading day can represent more than 30–50% of your total net profit (the exact threshold varies by firm). Many traders hit their profit target and still get rejected because one exceptional day blew past the consistency threshold. Zentrade calculates this in real time on the dashboard, with a visual alert when you're approaching the limit for your specific firm's rules. This alone has helped traders avoid costly rejections they never saw coming.
AI-Powered Revenge Trading Detection

This is the most significant functional difference between the two platforms. Zentrade's ZenMode plan includes an AI detection engine that monitors two revenge trading patterns: (1) entering a new trade within 30 minutes of closing a losing position, and (2) three or more consecutive losing trades in the same session. When detected, a dismissible alert banner appears on the dashboard, and affected calendar days are flagged with an orange badge. This creates accountability at exactly the moment it matters most — when emotions are highest. TradeZella has no equivalent feature at any price point.
Weekly AI Performance Report with Emotional Analysis

Every Monday morning, Zentrade Professional and ZenMode users receive an AI-generated report analyzing the previous week: P&L by session hour, emotional patterns from the trading journal, consistency percentage trends, and specific recommendations based on the trader's documented trading plan. The report factors in emotional tags logged during the week — stress, confidence, FOMO, hesitation — to surface behavioral correlations. This is not available in TradeZella.
Futures Instrument Support

Most prop firm evaluations for futures traders focus on CME instruments: NQ (Nasdaq-100 futures), ES (S&P 500 futures), CL (Crude Oil), GC (Gold), MES (Micro E-mini S&P 500), and MNQ (Micro E-mini Nasdaq-100). In Zentrade, these instruments are preconfigured with the correct contract multipliers, so your P&L calculations are accurate without any manual setup. In TradeZella, the setup for futures instruments requires more manual configuration, which can lead to calculation errors if done incorrectly.
Ease of Use and Daily Workflow

A trading journal only helps if you actually use it every day. Both platforms offer dark mode as the default interface. Zentrade's navigation is organized around the daily trader workflow: Accounts → Trades → Dashboard → Calendar → Trading Plan. The calendar view shows each day's P&L with emotional tags and revenge trading flags, making the week-at-a-glance review genuinely useful rather than decorative.

TradeZella has a polished interface that benefits from years of design iteration. Its broker integration for US stock accounts (TD Ameritrade, Interactive Brokers, Webull) is more mature for that specific use case. However, if you're a futures trader who imports trades via CSV from NinjaTrader, Tradovate, or Rithmic, Zentrade's import flow is specifically designed for those file formats.
Start with Zentrade for free and see how much clearer your trading patterns become.
Who Should Use Each Platform?
Zentrade is the better choice if...

You value weekly AI reports that connect your emotional state to your trading performance
Budget matters: starting at $9/month with real features versus $29/month minimum elsewhere

content.text: TradeZella may suit you better if...

items: You trade primarily stocks or options through US brokers with direct integration support
English is your primary working language and you don't need Spanish localization
You have months of historical data in TradeZella and the migration effort outweighs the benefit
You prioritize product maturity and established broker integrations over AI emotional features

content.text: Final Verdict: Zentrade vs TradeZella for Funded Traders

For futures traders pursuing funded accounts through prop firm evaluations, Zentrade wins this comparison on the metrics that matter most for that specific goal. The real-time consistency rule tracker, AI revenge trading detection, weekly emotional performance reports, and purpose-built futures instrument support are all features that directly affect whether you pass or fail an evaluation.

TradeZella is a capable general-purpose trading journal with strong broker integrations for the US stock market. But it was not built with the funded futures trader in mind. If passing a prop firm evaluation is your primary goal in 2025, Zentrade gives you the specific tools to get there — starting with a free account you can create right now.
Zentrade's free plan requires no credit card. You can track your trades, view your consistency metrics, and explore the dashboard before deciding whether to upgrade — zero financial risk.

faqItems.q: Does Zentrade have a free plan?
faqItems.a: Yes. Zentrade offers a genuinely functional free plan with no credit card required. It includes 1 account, manual trade entry, and a basic performance dashboard — enough to start tracking and improving during an evaluation.
No. TradeZella is an English-only platform with no Spanish language support. Zentrade offers full Spanish localization across the entire application.

faqItems.q: Which trading journal is better for FTMO evaluations?
faqItems.a: Zentrade is specifically designed for FTMO and other prop firm evaluations. Its consistency rule tracker, prop firm benchmark tools, and AI revenge trading detection are built around the specific rules that FTMO, Apex Trader Funding, and TopStep enforce.

faqItems.q: Does TradeZella have AI revenge trading detection?
faqItems.a: No. TradeZella does not offer AI-powered revenge trading detection at any price tier. Zentrade's ZenMode plan ($59/month) includes this feature as part of its AI suite.

faqItems.q: How much does Zentrade cost compared to TradeZella?
faqItems.a: Zentrade starts at $0 (free), with paid plans at $9, $29, and $59/month. TradeZella requires a paid subscription starting around $29/month for meaningful analytics, making Zentrade significantly more affordable at the entry level.

faqItems.q: Can I import my trades from NinjaTrader or Tradovate into Zentrade?
faqItems.a: Yes. Zentrade Professional and ZenMode plans include CSV auto-import with column mapping, designed to work with the export formats from NinjaTrader, Tradovate, Rithmic, and other popular futures platforms.

faqItems.q: What is the consistency rule and does Zentrade track it?
faqItems.a: The consistency rule is a prop firm requirement that no single trading day represents more than a set percentage (typically 30–50%) of your total net profit. Zentrade tracks this in real time and alerts you before you accidentally violate it — a feature TradeZella does not offer.
